proposed_base_material_variables.phase_volume_fractions: The specific volumetric percentages or ratios of distinct crystallographic phases present within the bulk material (e.g., 50:50 austenite-ferrite in duplex stainless steels), which explicitly govern phase-specific localized corrosion mechanisms and defect distributions.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.sensitization_status: An explicit indicator of whether the material was purposefully subjected to thermal conditions (e.g., held at intermediate temperatures like 650 °C) to induce grain boundary carbide precipitation and chromium depletion prior to testing.

proposed_experiment_variables.testing_standard_reference: The specifically cited international or industry testing guidelines (e.g., ASTM G.1) followed to standardize the electrochemical cell setup, electrode preparation, and overarching methodology.
proposed_experiment_variables.applied_constant_potential: The fixed electrochemical potential continuously applied to the working electrode during potentiostatic (constant-potential) tests, used to evaluate time-dependent localized corrosion initiation.
proposed_experiment_variables.potentiostatic_hold_duration: The predefined, continuous length of time for which a constant electrochemical potential is applied during potentiostatic testing before recording the terminal current density.
proposed_experiment_variables.potentiostatic_current_density: The specific current density recorded after holding the working electrode at a fixed constant potential for a designated duration, utilized to evaluate long-term localized pitting susceptibility.
